definition.name: Agentic Executables (AE)
definition.description: Libraries/packages managed by AI agents as executable programs for installation, configuration, usage, and uninstallation.

library.description: Maintain AE files within the library itself
library.use_case: For library maintainers creating AE documentation

project.description: Use AE in projects that depend on the library
project.use_case: For developers integrating libraries as AE

actions.name: bootstrap
actions.description: Create/maintain AE files in a library

actions.name: update
actions.description: Update AE to a newer version

actions.name: use
actions.description: Apply AE capabilities in the project

tools.name: ae_definition
tools.description: Retrieve core AE definition and framework overview
tools.use_case: Understand what AE is and which contexts/actions are available

tools.name: ae_instructions
tools.description: Retrieve contextual documentation for context+action combinations
tools.use_case: Get detailed instructions for a specific context and action

tools.name: ae_generate
tools.description: Generate AE files via codex or deterministic templates

tools.name: ae_registry
tools.description: Submit libraries to registry or fetch published AE artifacts

tools.name: ae_verify
tools.description: Generate verification checklist based on AE principles
tools.use_case: Verify implementation quality after making changes

tools.name: ae_evaluate
tools.description: Score implementation compliance with detailed feedback
tools.use_case: Objective compliance evaluation with actionable fixes

usage_guide.library_maintainers: Use instructions(context="library", action="bootstrap") to create AE files, then registry submit to publish.
usage_guide.project_developers: Use registry get to fetch ae_install/ae_update/ae_use and apply in project context.
usage_guide.workflow: After implementation run verify first and evaluate second for measurable quality checks.

core_principles.name: Agent Empowerment
core_principles.description: Equip AI agents with meta-rules to autonomously maintain, install, configure, integrate, use, and uninstall AEs based on project needs.

core_principles.name: Modularity
core_principles.description: Structure AE instructions in clear, reusable steps: Installation -> Configuration -> Integration -> Usage -> Uninstallation.

core_principles.name: Contextual Awareness
core_principles.description: Ensure AE documentation provides sufficient domain knowledge for agents to understand integration points without manual intervention.

core_principles.name: Reversibility
core_principles.description: Design uninstallation to cleanly remove all traces of the AE, restoring the original state.

core_principles.name: Validation
core_principles.description: Include checks for installation, configuration, and usage to ensure reliability and allow for corrections.

core_principles.name: Documentation Focus
core_principles.description: Prioritize concise, agent-readable instructions over verbose human-oriented docs.

data.message: AE v2 uses a shared core with CLI-first workflows and MCP as optional thin adapter.
